author: @EmmittBrownBTTF1

author: @raywharton9425
text:
Reminds me of ecology. What you can take out of an environment is what is in the environment minus what that environment needs to be what it is. Of course more can be taken out, and certain species do it all the time, at the moment where I live deer are doing so. It's just that you cannot taken that extra out and keep the existing system.

It occurs to me the the laws of thermo-dynamics happened along side an industrialism which was based on using resources in a way where once you use something it becomes rubbish. A one way path. They could get fabulous concentrations and intensities of energy by using a system in a way that doesn't bother with preserving the system that was put to work.

The amount of energy you can get out of a forest goes up considerably if the forest itself is an acceptable loss. On a certain time line, on a different scale of time the contrary is true.

To be the Gibb's reminds me of the idea of a niche. A species can use everything that flows into its niche as it pleases, with out 'working' just by doing it's thing. I think it is free because what ever is getting work done is always part of the system, we are always part of the environment.

So free energy where I live, the sun comes to mind, high elevation and low cloud cover gives intense sun and big thermal differences between night and day (bigger contrasts is good omen when hunting energy!). In many places I think plants do sun best, they have far far far more expertise than us by billions of years it would seem. Self assembling solar panels that can fit in a mustard seed! But they need water, which is rare here, while I have ways of bringing that energy into the cycle that require little water. Wind too is good. But wood is not so good, because its already busy doing other work, casting cool shade, slowing the drying winds while standing, and holding the precious water while lying down.

author: @brixomatic
text: I'm currently enjoying your videos as food for thought right now, as you might notice from the amount of comments I'm making. When you said that the laws actually don't restrict how things are working, but rather describe them, which would leave some liberties, it came through my mind, that I'd like to add that you can't of course just look at one law on it's own. Asking the question if could one particular law be interpreted in another way, would that interpretation collide with another law? Or could other laws work the way they do, if the one law could be interpreted in the way that I'm trying to at the moment? I hope you get the point of my helpless way to describe things. To put it bluntly: Newton's law of F=m*a would not restrict the acceleration of a mass at all. Taken enough time and space the tiniest force would be sufficient to accelerate any mass beyond speed of light, this is of course only when you ignore all the laws around it, let alone special relativity.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I think I get what you are saying. But, my point is that laws are too often used to prescribe events rather than describing them. If we look at an event that can never be self contradictory as it just is. But laws as imperfect understanding frequently are. I am glad you are enjoying the food for thought because you raise interesting points and my ideas are far from complete

author: @RixDobbs
text: Thank you for clarifying the second law of thermodynamics. But you missed my question that if this is so, how is it that refrigerators and air conditioners work moving heat from one box out to the world at large at a higher temperature? Moving heat from a lower temperature into a higher temperature situation?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: sorry about that mate i see what your saying. Per the law It is not possible for heat to flow from a colder body to a warmer body spontaneously This precludes a perfect refrigerator as energy will not flow spontaneously from a low temperature object to a higher temperature object. However, the Clausius statement of the law allows this if work is being done and it is the work being done that accomplishes this flow.

author: @RixDobbs
text: Remind me again what the second law of thermodynamics IS; Matter cannot be created nor destroyed but can only change form, or is it that energy only flows from a more energetic state to a lower energetic state? Or have I missed it completely. If it is stated that heat only flows from the hotter tho the colder and never the other way around - How is it that a refrigerator or an air conditioner works? Is the second law only a suggestion? ...   Love your work, by the way.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: The second law of thermodynamics states that the entropy of an isolated system never decreases, because isolated systems spontaneously evolve toward thermodynamic equilibrium—the state of maximum entropy. The law you quote is the conservation of matter. The first law of thermodynamics is a version of the law of conservation of energy, adapted for thermodynamic systems. The law of conservation of energy states that the total energy of an isolated system is constant; energy can be transformed from one form to another, but cannot be created or destroyed.. Energy flow is a corollary of the second law really and is often expressed as time's arrow

author: @MathewTitus
text: Quite honestly, I've NEVER understood Gibbs Free Energy. But as for Entropy - I think of it in terms of Information Theory. Very roughly, I think of it as a measure of the amount of Information needed to describe a system EXACTLY. The Greater the Disorder - the Longer the Description. That's my view anyway. (My research is on Complexity, which is tied up to Information Theory using Gell-Mann's definition of Effective Complexity. Life, for example - is one form of Effective Complexity.)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: The problem with entropy is its constructional - as i am sure we both know the concept of informational entropy was introduced to cope with Maxwell's demon you don't need the caps mate lol
